title: Print Warning message if cmd will not be used based on entrypoint

comments.body: This fixes an issue brought up in this https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/736
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 7cc1292) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

would it make sense to have this behaviour only with `buildah bud` (where we can more easilty check if CMD was already done earlier) for compatibility with Docker but let `buildah --entrypoint` override the ENTRYPOINT everytime as it does now?  I didn't know of this behaviour, but it looks quite confusing.
Well it will override the behaviour now, the only concern is what happens to the CMD field.
Let me make a series of Docker tests to confirm what it does. I am not 100% convinced wiping CMD is the right thing to do. The PR it was resolving had another issue with entrypoint.

All the following are executed with `docker build` / `docker inspect`
**entrypoint:src**
```
FROM centos
CMD /entrypoint.src
```

Produces:
```
            "Cmd": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/command.src"
            ],

            "Entrypoint": null,
```

Building from the above container:

**entrypoint:samecmd** (child same CMD as parent, introduces ENTRYPOINT)
```
FROM entrypoint:src
CMD /command.src
ENTRYPOINT /entrypoint.new
```

```
            "Cmd": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/command.src"
            ],
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/entrypoint.new"
            ],
```

**entrypoint:diffcmd** (child different CMD to parent, introduces ENTRYPOINT)
```
FROM entrypoint:src
CMD /command.new
ENTRYPOINT /entrypoint.new
```

```
            "Cmd": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/command.new"
            ],
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/entrypoint.new"
            ],
```

**entrypoint:nocmd** (child no CMD, introduces ENTRYPOINT)
```
FROM entrypoint:src
ENTRYPOINT /entrypoint.new
```

```
            "Cmd": null,
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/entrypoint.new"
            ],
```

In summary, the only situation where the `CMD` is blanked by entrypoint, is when the parent container contains `CMD`, the child doesn't have a `CMD` specified but does specify an `ENTRYPOINT`

Not sure where I sit with `buildah --config` blanking `CMD` when a user can explicitly blank it themselves if that's what they want. It feels like a destructive default option. If that option stays, could there perhaps be a warning eg.

```
buildah config --entrypoint /entrypoint $cid
Note: CMD entry '/command' was found in the container and removed 
```
bot, retest this please
@pixdrift @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL

@rhatdan, looks good, just want your thoughts on the additional message. Wanted to display that message because at that point, the behaviour of the CMD changes.

Really like the code re-org.. 👍
